subject: Ecology
title: Decomposer Fungi
description: A short lesson to help you apply Decomposer Fungi.

hook.text: Without fungi, dead trees would pile up forever. Nothing else can break down lignin.

buildup.text: White-rot fungi produce enzymes that dismantle lignin—wood's toughest structural polymer.

discovery.text: Brown-rot fungi attack cellulose, leaving behind the brown, crumbly residue of rotting wood.

twist.text: Before fungi evolved to digest lignin, dead trees accumulated for millions of years as coal.

climax.text: Decomposer fungi close the carbon cycle, returning locked-up carbon to the soil and air.

punchline.text: Fungi invented wood recycling 300 million years ago.

quiz.question: What happened before fungi could decompose lignin?
options: Dead trees accumulated as coal deposits
Soil was too acidic for any growth
